---
title: "Rāg Bṛndāvanī sāraṅg"
subtitle: "*Jaya prabala begavati*"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
---

::: {.composer-info}
**Composer:** Raṅganāth &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Tāl:** Tīvrā (Madhyalay)
:::


## Lyrics {.unnumbered}

::: {.lyrics-block}

**Sthāyī**

> jaya prabala begavati sureśvari jayati jaya gaṅge  
> trijagata tāriṇi jaga kaluṣa nāśini pāravati  
> raṅganātha suta para neka karahara tapana suta bhara antime

**Antarā**

> tua nīra niramala karata ḍhala ḍhala tīra taṭa ati śobhini  
> naga nandini itha makara dinakara candrimāghame dehi pada yuga bhāgame

:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" collapse="true"}
## Reading the notation

Tīvrā is a 7-beat cycle divided as **3 + 2 + 2** (vibhāgs marked **X**, **2**, **3**). Each row below shows **two complete cycles**: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–7 of the first cycle and 1–7 of the second cycle, with sam **X** at beats 1 and 8, **2** at beats 4 and 11, and **3** at beats 6 and 13. 

The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash **-** indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets $\lb{\cdot}$ denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts $\sps{\cdot}$ indicate the starting grace note of a glide; $\tr{X}$ denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); $\mn{X}$ denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).
:::

## Sthāyī {.unnumbered}

::: {.notation-table .doubletivra}

| **X** |   |   | **2** |   | **3** |   | **X** |   |   | **2** |   | **3** |   |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| - | - | - | - | - | S | S | R | m | m | P | N | N | N |
| **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **ja** | **ya** | **pra** | **ba** | **la** | **be** | **-** | **ga** | **va** |
| $\tr{S}$ | - | - | - | - | S | S | R | m | m | P | N | N | N |
| **ti** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **ja** | **ya** | **pra** | **ba** | **la** | **be** | **-** | **ga** | **va** |
| $\tr{S}$ | - | N | $\tr{S}$ | - | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | N | P | P | N | - |
| **ti** | **-** | **su** | **re** | **-** | **śva** | **ri** | **ja** | **ya** | **ti** | **ja** | **ya** | **ga** | **-** |
| N | $\tr{S}$ | - | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\lb{N\tr{S}}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{R}$ | - | $\tr{S}$ | N |
| **ṅge** | **-** | **-** | **tri** | **ja** | **ga** | **ta** | **tā** | **-** | **ri** | **ṇi** | **-** | **ja** | **ga** |
| $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | N | P | - | m | P | $\lb{mR}$ | - | R | S | - | S | S |
| **ka** | **lu** | **ṣa** | **nā** | **-** | **śi** | **ni** | **pā** | **-** | **rava** | **tī** | **-** | **ra** | **ṅga** |
| R | m | m | m | P | P | P | $\lb{\sps{P}N}$ | P | P | m | P | N | N |
| **nā** | **-** | **tha** | **su** | **ta** | **pa** | **ra** | **ne** | **-** | **ka** | **ka** | **ra** | **ha** | **ra** |
| N | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | N | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{S}$ | - | N | P | - | m | m |
| **ta** | **pa** | **na** | **su** | **ta** | **bha** | **ra** | **a** | **-** | **nti** | **me** | **-** | **ja** | **ya** |

:::

## Antarā {.unnumbered}

::: {.notation-table .doubletivra}

| **X** |   |   | **2** |   | **3** |   | **X** |   |   | **2** |   | **3** |   |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| - | - | - | - | - | N | N | N | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ |
| **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **-** | **tu** | **a** | **nī** | **-** | **ra** | **ni** | **ra** | **ma** | **la** |
| $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{S}$ | N | N | P | N | N | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ |
| **ka** | **ra** | **ta** | **ḍha** | **la** | **ḍha** | **la** | **nī** | **-** | **ra** | **ni** | **ra** | **ma** | **la** |
| $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | N | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{S}$ |
| **ka** | **ra** | **ta** | **ḍha** | **la** | **ḍha** | **la** | **tī** | **-** | **ra** | **ta** | **ṭa** | **a** | **ti** |
| $\lb{N\tr{S}}$ | $\tr{R}$ | $\tr{S}$ | $\tr{R}$ | - | $\tr{S}$ | N | N | $\tr{S}$ | N | $\tr{S}$ | - | m | m |
| **śo** | **-** | **bhi** | **ni** | **-** | **na** | **ga** | **na** | **-** | **ndi** | **ni** | **-** | **i** | **tha** |
| P | N | N | $\tr{S}$ | N | $\tr{S}$ | R | $\tr{S}$ | - | $\tr{S}$ | N | - | P | m |
| **ma** | **ka** | **ra** | **di** | **na** | **ka** | **ra** | **ca** | **-** | **ndri** | **mā** | **-** | **gha** | **me** |
| P | $\tr{S}$ | N | P | P | m | P | m | R | R | S | - | - | - |
| **de** | **-** | **hi** | **pa** | **da** | **yu** | **ga** | **bhā** | **-** | **ga** | **me** | **-** | **-** | **-** |

:::
